title: Helictotrichon aetolicum
taxonomy: Helictotrichon aetolicum is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It is classified as a member of the genus Helictotrichon, which is a group of grasses known for their tufted habit and hair-like inflorescences. The specific epithet 'aetolicum' likely refers to the region of Aetolia in Greece, where the plant is native. This species is often grown in gardens for its ornamental value due to its striking foliage and ability to thrive in a variety of conditions. Helictotrichon aetolicum is often used as a ornamental grass in Mediterranean and alpine-style gardens.

It is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of digestive disorders and improve immune function.
